summary: Backend-only review completed for the C++ Licensing module using static code inspection and limited toolchain validation. No runtime tests executed: cmake is missing in container, and all Python backend pytest cases were skipped because NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL/REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is not configured. Success rates are therefore static-review only.

critical.issue: LicenseManager is constructed in PluginProcessor, but no call sites invoke loadCachedCertificate/activateWithKey/importOfflineCertificate/clearActivation in plugin flow. Runtime licensing state may remain Unknown/ActivationRequired unless external/UI code (not found in repo search) triggers it.

minor.issue: cmake not installed in environment, so CMake wiring and unit test build could not be executed here.
Direct g++ syntax check fails without JUCE include paths generated by CMake/FetchContent, so compile verification stayed conceptual.
All pytest cases skipped due to missing NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL/REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Skipped tests are not counted as passed.

integration_issues.issue: MOCKED: StubActivationClient returns not_configured for activate/refresh/deactivate by design; real licensing backend is not wired.

action_items: Wire a startup/license-init path (e.g., plugin init or editor init) that calls LicenseManager::loadCachedCertificate() and publishes runtime state deterministically.
Add integration/unit test coverage proving cached certificate load and runtime state publication happen without manual trigger.
Run full CMake configure + build + Catch2 tests in an environment with cmake and JUCE toolchain available.
Provide runtime API env values and rerun Python backend pytest; do not count skipped tests as passed.
critical_code_review_comments: PluginProcessor.h is 695 lines and PluginProcessor.cpp is 2246 lines (>700 guideline); split licensing orchestration and non-audio-thread orchestration into focused modules.
LicenseVerifier development signature path is intentionally non-cryptographic string match; ensure production verifier replacement is mandatory before release.

success_rate.methodology: Weighted sum per track: 35% static code review, 15% local syntax/compile checks, 25% unit tests, 25% integration tests. Skipped tests score 0.0, not 1.0. Scores are now split into Python backend and C++/JUCE plugin tracks because the previous single 'backend' figure mixed unrelated artifacts.

python_backend.notes: All pytest cases skipped (0/2 executed). No runtime API validation performed.

cpp_plugin.notes: Only static review performed. CMake and Catch2 runtime blocked by missing cmake in container.

context_for_next_testing_agent: No prior /app/test_reports existed. /app/backend/tests also does not exist (this is C++ Catch2 test layout under /app/tests). Verified licensing files are present and included in CMake source sets; TestLicensing.cpp exists and covers key parser + verifier basics. Could not execute CMake/tests due missing cmake in container. Skipped pytest cases must not be counted as passed.
rca of the issue: RCA (primary): Runtime licensing state integration gap. Repro: grep across /app/src shows LicenseManager methods (loadCachedCertificate/activateWithKey/importOfflineCertificate/clearActivation) are defined but have no call sites in plugin/editor flows; only getter exists on PluginProcessor. Impact: certificate may be valid on disk but runtime state not loaded unless future UI path manually triggers. Mitigation: invoke loadCachedCertificate during safe non-audio initialization and add integration test asserting state transitions on startup.
